data.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S4 30b3: Attested signing — pin verified_at_sha via the signed manifest channel); 4-bucket audit against the WHOLE EIGHT-child roster]

VERDICT: EMPTY G3 findings list. The union of the eight children (S1 6d56 materialize core, S2 70c7 cache read/populate, S2b b73c janitor, S3 fa49 gate threading, S4 30b3 THIS attested signing, S4b e308 claim-gate ref-coherence/ADR 0002, S5 b7fa CLI/MCP surfaces, S6 1ba5 docs) covers every parent acceptance/success criterion; no uncovered parent criterion is attributable to this pairing. Every absence below was cross-checked against the WHOLE roster before being dismissed.

WHAT THIS CHILD IS: S4 is the attested-signing slice — the PRIMARY/SOLE owner of binding verified_at_sha into the signed bytes via the EXISTING manifest channel (a `verified-at-sha:<sha>` manifest step, NOT a payload-schema change), plus the queryable field + lifecycle, the resolution boundary + fail-closed, the CAS-serialized atomic/idempotent/overwrite pin, the local-mode signing bar, and the in-toto envelope shape. It is the heart of parent AC2 and a co-owner of AC3 (signing bar) and AC8 (payload-schema-SHA-binding rejected alternative in the ADR).

4-bucket coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; THIS child's role + bucket):

- Parent AC1 (no code-reading gate reads the server working tree in attested mode; all read a faithful snapshot at the pinned SHA) — FULLY by S1 (materialize core) + S3 (gate re-rooting). Bucket STRUCTURAL w.r.t. THIS child (its Scope: "Out: snapshot/cache (S1/S2)"); it signs over the pinned SHA but does not perform the snapshot read. Not an absence.

- Parent AC2 (a signed verdict binds verified_at_sha via the signed MANIFEST CHANNEL — no payload-schema/version change, prior signatures still verify — and is reproducible against that SHA) — FULLY covered, with THIS child as PRIMARY/SOLE OWNER: its AC1 (attested PASS adds a `verified-at-sha:<sha>` MANIFEST STEP covered by the signature; verifying certifies the SHA; NO `_canonical_payload` change, NO PAYLOAD_VERSION bump; regression test pre-existing signatures still verify; decision recorded in the ADR) + its AC2 (verified_at_sha queryable field; re-running against the same SHA reproduces a verdict + manifest over identical content; head_sha audit field preserved/additive). Every clause of parent AC2 — manifest-channel binding, no payload bump, prior-signature non-breakage, reproducibility — maps to a concrete AC here. Bucket FULLY. Not an absence; this is the heart of the child.

- Parent AC3 (local source mode reads the in-place checkout AND cannot produce a signed verdict; source=local is the documented back-out) — covered SPLIT: S3 (fa49 AC4) owns the "reads in-place checkout" + "documented back-out" half; THIS child owns the "CANNOT produce a signed verdict" enforcement half — its AC4 ("a `local`-mode run (dirty allowed) never sets [verified_at_sha] and never emits a signature"). Clean reader/enforcer split; no uncovered half. Bucket PARTIALLY (signing-bar half here). Not an absence.

- Parent AC4 (concurrent gates on same/different SHAs without git-lock contention/cross-op corruption; cache + janitor reclaim disk under a defined free-space watermark (S2) without disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY by S1 (atomic/no-worktree population) + S2 (single-flight, delete-on-last-close) + S2b (janitor watermark reclamation). Note: THIS child's AC3 introduces its OWN concurrency obligation (CAS-serialized pin + verified_at_sha write so two writers racing / a re-verify against a different SHA cannot interleave or leave a stale/double pin) — that is the SIGNING-side concurrency safety, distinct from and additive to the git/cache concurrency of parent AC4, and it is wholly owned here. Bucket STRUCTURAL w.r.t. the git/cache half of AC4. Not an absence.

- Parent AC5 (private-repo fetch failure surfaces a descriptive, actionable credential error) — FULLY by S1 (raises the actionable error) + S6 (documents message + remedy). Note: THIS child's AC5 (an unresolvable ref in attested mode fails closed — no signature) is the SIGNING-side resolution boundary, adjacent to but distinct from the FETCH-credential error of parent AC5; the credential-error obligation itself is owned by S1+S6. Bucket STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child for AC5's credential clause. Not an absence.

- Parent AC6 (portable across macOS + Linux: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY by S1 (implementation) + S6 (docs). Bucket STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child (out of its scope). Not an absence.

- Parent AC7 (operational tunables — free-space watermark, default ref, grace/age windows — configurable with documented defaults, S2/S3) — watermark/grace/age owned by S2b (b73c AC9); default ref/source owned by S3 (fa49); docs by S6. THIS child consumes the default ref ("resolve the client `ref` (default `origin/main`)") but does not own the tunable mechanism. Bucket STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.

- Parent AC8 (an ADR captures the architecture + rejected alternatives — PID-lease, payload-schema SHA binding) — co-owned: the PID-lease rejection + cache/janitor architecture is S2/S2b (b73c AC10); the **payload-schema SHA-binding** rejected alternative is PRECISELY THIS child's contribution — its AC1 records "the manifest-vs-payload signing decision ... in the architecture ADR" (i.e. why the SHA is bound via the manifest channel rather than a new signed-payload/`_canonical_payload` field). The parent's ADR section explicitly says the manifest-channel SHA binding is "authored in S2/S4." Both named rejected alternatives have an authoring home; this child covers the payload-schema-SHA-binding clause. Bucket PARTIALLY (co-owner of the AC8 rejected-alternatives set). Not an absence. (ADR 0002 — the code-drift ADR — is a DISTINCT artifact owned by S4b e308; not to be confused with the architecture ADR of parent AC8.)

- Parent AC9 (all EIGHT enumerated child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by the existence of all eight children (this is S4 of 8) + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Success-criteria narrative ("Every code-reading gate verifies a client-specified, immutable, reproducible snapshot at a pinned SHA — never the server's mutable checkout — safely under concurrent distributed use; signed verdicts bind verified_at_sha via the manifest channel (reproducible, no prior-signature breakage); local mode preserves the single-developer verify-before-push workflow") decomposes onto AC1-AC3/AC8. The MIDDLE clause — "signed verdicts bind verified_at_sha via the manifest channel (reproducible, no prior-signature breakage)" — is verbatim this child's core deliverable (its AC1/AC2). The "never the server's mutable checkout / immutable pinned-SHA basis" clause is S1/S3; "local preserves verify-before-push" is S3's in-place read + THIS child's signing bar (AC4). No uncovered obligation introduced by this child's existence.

Named-consumer / delegation cross-checks (covered-by-named-consumer, not absences):
- This child's Scope explicitly delegates the claim-gate ref-resolution coherence OUT to sibling S4b (e308) ("Out: claim-gate ref-resolution coherence (sibling story)"). S4b IS present in the roster and CLAIMS it (e308 AC1: plan-review file_impact hashing + claim-gate freshness re-check resolve through ONE shared boundary at the same pinned-SHA basis). The reproducibility sub-obligation of parent AC2 that depends on the signed dependency map and the later freshness re-check hashing at the SAME basis is therefore covered by a named sibling — not orphaned.
- snapshot/cache (S1/S2) delegated out and owned by S1/S2/S2b; HMAC-trust-model docs delegated to S6 ("limits documented in S6"), claimed by S6 AC4.

Note (scope, NOT a G3 finding): the standing G4 dependency-gap on THIS child (the three sign/verify-side hashing sites — attest.py dependency_hashes()/_rehash()/the ADR-0002 freshness re-check — rooting on _config.repo_root, with neither this child's nor S4b's file_impact naming src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py) is a CONSISTENCY (G4) concern about WHICH module the manifest pin + shared resolution boundary actually land in and WHO routes the snapshot handle there. It is NOT a G3 union-coverage absence: the manifest-channel SHA-binding obligation IS claimed by THIS child (AC1/AC2) and the ref-coherence obligation IS claimed by S4b (AC1). A criterion that is claimed by a child is covered for G3 purposes; the where-does-it-land question belongs in the G4 channel for this pairing.

ANTI-FP cross-check on the one plausible absence: could parent AC2's "reproducible against that SHA" be left UNCOVERED because this child binds the SHA in the manifest but the verdict body might still be computed against the mutable checkout? Checked the whole roster — NO: the snapshot-only read basis is claimed by S1+S3 (AC1 re-rooting + the AC7 wrong-branch-invariance regression test), and the residual attestation-hashing/ref-coherence path is claimed by S4b (AC1) with a negative test guarding whole-HEAD divergence. The obligation is claimed across the roster; the only open question (which module the routing lands in) is G4, not a G3 absence.

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the attested-signing slice — it is the PRIMARY/SOLE OWNER of parent AC2 (manifest-channel verified_at_sha binding, no payload bump, prior signatures verify, reproducible), the signing-bar half of AC3, and the payload-schema-SHA-binding rejected-alternative clause of AC8; and it contributes its own signing-side concurrency safety (CAS pin) and fail-closed resolution boundary. Every parent acceptance/success criterion has a named owner in the eight-story roster; none is left uncovered. NO G3 finding for this pairing.
